# goal-cli

> Artifact-first control for coding agents. Configure one canonical artifact,
> rebuild it with a producer command, let tik judge the artifact, then let tok
> repair only bounded source scopes.

`goal-cli` is for users who can judge the finished output but do not want to
audit every code diff. The most important setup task is creating a reliable
producer command that rebuilds the canonical artifact from source.

Copy-paste prompt for an agent:

```text
You are configuring this repository for goal-cli.

Use the goal-cli-project-setup skill from skills/goal-cli-project-setup/SKILL.md.
Discover the canonical artifact, synthesize a stable producer command or wrapper,
write or update goal.toml, protect generated outputs, keep tok write scopes
narrow, and run validation.

Required checks:
- run the producer directly and prove the artifact exists;
- run goal-cli validate;
- run goal-cli doctor;
- run goal-cli run --dry-run;
- run Codex smoke checks only if the configured providers require them.

Do not edit raw data, generated artifacts, .git/, or .goal/. Ask only if the
canonical artifact cannot be inferred safely. Finish by reporting the artifact
path, producer command, tik provider, tok write_dirs, generated_dirs, and the
next exact command to run.
```

## Agent Entry Points

- [Project setup skill](skills/goal-cli-project-setup/SKILL.md): Use this to
  connect an existing project to goal-cli, including producer synthesis.
- [Template author skill](skills/goal-cli-template-author/SKILL.md): Use this
  to maintain reusable templates, oracle scripts, examples, and docs.

## Core Documentation

- [README](README.md): Product overview, quick start, runtime loop, and
  command deck.
- [Installation](docs/installation.md): Local install, dependencies,
  no-mistakes gate, observability, and first run.
- [goal.toml schema](docs/config-schema.md): Full configuration reference for
  artifact, producer, tik, tok, safety, and observability.
- [CLI reference](docs/cli-reference.md): Current command help surfaces.
- [Skills guide](docs/skills.md): How to choose, install, and use the goal-cli
  skills.

## Examples

- [PDF-first example goal](examples/scientificity/goal.toml):
  Research-paper style goal using a PDF artifact, `codex_file` tik, and
  `codex_goal` tok.

## Optional

- [Artifact-centered design notes](docs/artifact-goal-notes.md): Rationale for
  the producer -> tik -> tok heartbeat model.
- [Codex goal implementation report](docs/codex-goal-openai-implementation-report.md):
  Implementation details for Codex `/goal` tok integration.
